<h2>Supplemental Information: Episode 1</h2>

  <div class="supplement" id="e5.1">
    <h3 class="supplement-title">SMT-Pod Student Seminar Series: Gender and Contemporary Opera (Introduction)</h3>
    <h4>Rachel Lumsden (Florida State University)</h4>
    <h5>Release Date: Thursday, April 23, 2026</h5>
    <div class="floatsupps">
      <div style="float:left; width: 40%;">
        <img class="biopic" alt="Lumsden Headshot" src="../supplements/S05Ep01lumsden.jpg">

        <p style="clear:both; padding-top: 10px;"><a href="../supplements/S05Ep01Supp_lumsden.pdf" target="_blank">Supplemental Materials (PDF)</a>
        </p>

        <p style="font-size: 12px;">

        <strong>Team Lead:</strong> Megan Lyons<br/>

        <p style="font-size: 12px;">
        <span style="color:#de4d00;"><strong>Music Credits</strong></span><br/>

      </div>
      <div style="float:right; width: 60%;">
        <p><strong>Bio:</strong> Rachel Lumsden is Associate Professor of Music Theory at Florida State University. Her research examines issues of gender, race, and sexuality in contemporary British and American concert music. She has also published articles on public music theory and pedagogy, and co-edited <em>The Norton Guide to Teaching Music Theory</em> (2018). She recently won a university-wide award at FSU for Outstanding Graduate Teaching.</p>

        <p><strong>Keywords:</strong> <em>opera, gender, pedagogy, Laura Kaminsky, Thea Musgrave, Ellen Reid, Judith Weir</em></p>
      </div>
